title: cyathea saparuensis
intro: Cyathea saparuensis, also known as the New Caledonian tree fern, is a species of tree fern native to the island of New Caledonia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Cyathea saparuensis is often used in the form of extracts, teas, or infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
